name: Star Forts
description: Star forts, also known as bastion forts, are fortifications characterized by star-shaped layouts, designed to eliminate dead ground and provide interlocking fields of fire. Mainstream history attributes their development to the Italian Renaissance, evolving in response to the increasing power of cannons. Proponents of alternative theories suggest a different origin, claiming that many star forts predate the accepted timeline and possess advanced, lost technologies or were built by a vanished civilization. They often point to the seemingly global distribution of these forts, regardless of geographical barriers or historical records of technological exchange, as evidence of a unified, sophisticated ancient culture. These theories suggest the forts may have served purposes beyond defense, such as energy collection or manipulation (often linked to concepts like free energy or atmospheric electricity), or were built on pre-existing ley lines or power grids. Evidence cited includes geometric alignments, alleged advanced construction techniques not easily replicated with known technologies of the time, and similarities in design across continents. Mainstream historians and engineers attribute the global distribution to the spread of European military engineering during the Age of Exploration and colonization. They explain the construction techniques as variations on known methods, adapted to local materials and conditions. The perceived 'advanced' aspects are often explained by a lack of detailed understanding of historical engineering practices or an overestimation of the technological capabilities required for their construction. The geometric alignments are often dismissed as coincidental or related to practical military considerations, like maximizing fields of fire or adapting to terrain.
subtitle: Bastion forts and alternative theories

claims.claim: Star forts are evidence of a lost, advanced civilization.
claims.evidence: Global distribution, advanced construction techniques, geometric alignments.
claims.criticism: Distribution explained by European colonization; construction techniques are known variations; alignments are coincidental or practical military considerations.

claims.claim: Star forts were built using technologies beyond the capabilities of the attributed builders.
claims.evidence: Precision of construction, use of unusual materials, alleged energy-related functions.
claims.criticism: Precision achievable with known surveying and construction methods; materials are locally sourced and understood; 'energy-related functions' are speculative without concrete evidence.

claims.claim: Star forts served purposes beyond military defense, such as energy collection or manipulation.
claims.evidence: Geometric shapes, alleged connections to ley lines, claims of atmospheric electricity manipulation.
claims.criticism: Geometric shapes are for military effectiveness; 'ley lines' lack scientific validation; atmospheric electricity manipulation lacks evidence and contradicts established physics.

keyFigures.name: Carmen Boulter
keyFigures.role: Promotes theories about star forts' pre-Columbian origins and advanced technology.

keyFigures.name: Ben Davidson
keyFigures.role: Promotes theories about star forts as energy-collecting devices.

keyFigures.name: David Adelman
keyFigures.role: Historian specializing in military architecture, provides mainstream perspective on star fort design and purpose.

mythologyConnections: Giants as builders of ancient structures
Connections to geomancy and earth energies

timeline.event: Construction of star forts worldwide, attributed to European military expansion.
Emergence of alternative theories about star forts' origins and purpose.
Proliferation of star fort theories online, fueled by social media and alternative history communities.

sources.author: Duffy, Christopher
sources.work: Fire and Stone: The Science of Fortress Warfare 1660-1860
sources.title: Duffy, Christopher
